---
title: "Lecture 06 Notes: Robot Modeling III - Velocity Kinematics and Inverse Kinematics"
---

## Lecture slide

- [Lecture 06 slides](../slides/06-robot-modeling-2-velocity-jacobians.qmd)

## Key ideas

- Velocity kinematics links joint velocity to task-space velocity.
- The Jacobian captures local motion sensitivity at each configuration.
- Inverse kinematics can be solved iteratively using Jacobian-based updates.

## Core relation

\[
\dot{x} = J(q)\dot{q}, \quad
\dot{q} \approx J(q)^{\dagger}\dot{x}_{des}
\]

## Review questions

1. What does a Jacobian singularity mean physically?
2. Why is \(J^\dagger\) useful for redundant systems?
3. How does damping improve numerical IK near singularities?
